title: preprocess ".in" suffixed Dockerfiles
body:
Use CPP(1) on any input Dockerfile with a ".in" suffix.  This allows to
decompose Dockerfiles and make them reusable via the `#include`
directive.  Notice that those Dockerfiles can still be used by other
tools by manually running `cpp -E` on them.

This change affects both, the `imagebuild.BuildDockerfiles(...)` API and
buildah-bud.

Signed-off-by: Valentin Rothberg <vrothberg@suse.com>

comments.body: No tests yet as I am not sure how complex scenarios we should check.

Very nice.
Needs update buildah-bud.md.  Probably adding information to tuturial would be nice, although not required for this PR.
Also need a requires /usr/bin/cpp in the spec file, although I think we will do a recommends in the Fedora packages.
Thanks @vrothberg, looks really good overall.

@rhatdan @TomSweeneyRedHat Thanks! I updated the PR with tests and extended a bit the manpages. One thing I missed before was setting the context directory of buildah-bud as CPP's include path, so the basic requirement is that all includes are relative to the build-context directory.
